text.title: Spec Structure Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spec Structure. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Spec contains seven sections in order: Problem, Users, Goals, Non-goals, Requirements, Acceptance, Open Questions.
rule.rationale: Predictable order cuts reviewer scan time.

rule.statement: Sections marked N/A only with one-line justification; blanks rejected.
Core spec fits one page (≤700 words); longer means split.
rule.rationale: Specs over one page hide complexity that belongs in design.md.
Absent non-goals are the #1 source of scope creep.

rule.statement: Acceptance criteria are binary checks (Given/When/Then or rubric).
Open Questions section lists ≥1 question or 'none'; blank rejected.
rule.rationale: Hidden open questions surprise the impl-plan.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spec-structure.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
